 * value when the rule type is not `Explicit`.

Lines.FullText: data_rule_explicit Explicit rule

Elements.RawText: data_rule_explicit Explicit rule

Lines.FullText: When the rule type of the Data rule is set to `Explicit`, the values passed through the signal path, described by

Elements.RawText: When the rule type of the Data rule is set to `Explicit`, the values passed through the signal path, described by

Lines.FullText: the Value descriptor are stored in packet buffers.

Elements.RawText: the Value descriptor are stored in packet buffers.

Lines.FullText: The Explicit rule can have 2 optional parameters:

Elements.RawText: The Explicit rule can have 2 optional parameters:

Tags.RawText:
*
 * The Explicit rule can have 2 optional parameters:

Lines.FullText: - `minExpectedDelta`: Specifies the minimum difference in value between two subsequent samples

Elements.RawText: - `minExpectedDelta`: Specifies the minimum difference in value between two subsequent samples

Lines.FullText: - `maxExpectedDelta`: Specifies the maximum difference in value between two subsequent samples

Elements.RawText: - `maxExpectedDelta`: Specifies the maximum difference in value between two subsequent samples

Tags.RawText:
*
 * - `minExpectedDelta`: Specifies the minimum difference in value between two subsequent samples
 * - `maxExpectedDelta`: Specifies the maximum difference in value between two subsequent samples

Lines.FullText: These are mostly used for domain signals to specify the expected rate of a signal, or the expected timeout of a signal.

Elements.RawText: These are mostly used for domain signals to specify the expected rate of a signal, or the expected timeout of a signal.

Lines.FullText: The delta parameters should be configured to match the deltas in terms of the raw signal values (before scaling/resolution

Elements.RawText: The delta parameters should be configured to match the deltas in terms of the raw signal values (before scaling/resolution

Lines.FullText: are applied).

Elements.RawText: are applied).

Tags.RawText:
*
 * These are mostly used for domain signals to specify the expected rate of a signal, or the expected timeout of a signal.
 * The delta parameters should be configured to match the deltas in terms of the raw signal values (before scaling/resolution
 * are applied).

Lines.FullText: An explicit rule must have either both or none of these parameters. To use only one, the other must be set to 0.

Elements.RawText: An explicit rule must have either both or none of these parameters. To use only one, the other must be set to 0.

Tags.RawText:
*
 * An explicit rule must have either both or none of these parameters. To use only one, the other must be set to 0.

Lines.FullText: data_rule_implicit Implicit rule

Elements.RawText: data_rule_implicit Implicit rule

Lines.FullText: When the rule type of the Data rule is not `Explicit`, the buffers of packets are empty. The values must instead be

Elements.RawText: When the rule type of the Data rule is not `Explicit`, the buffers of packets are empty. The values must instead be

Lines.FullText: calculated via the Implicit value found in the packet buffers in conjunction with the parameters of the rule. Each

Elements.RawText: calculated via the Implicit value found in the packet buffers in conjunction with the parameters of the rule. Each

Lines.FullText: implicit rule type specifies its own required set of parameters.

Elements.RawText: implicit rule type specifies its own required set of parameters.

Tags.RawText:
@subsection data_rule_implicit Implicit rule
 * When the rule type of the Data rule is not `Explicit`, the buffers of packets are empty. The values must instead be
 * calculated via the Implicit value found in the packet buffers in conjunction with the parameters of the rule. Each
 * implicit rule type specifies its own required set of parameters.

Lines.FullText: data_rule_linear Linear rule

Elements.RawText: data_rule_linear Linear rule

Lines.FullText: The parameters include a `delta` and `start` integer members. The values are calculated according to the following

Elements.RawText: The parameters include a `delta` and `start` integer members. The values are calculated according to the following

Lines.FullText: equation: packetOffset + sampleIndex * delta + start.

Elements.RawText: equation: packetOffset + sampleIndex * delta + start.

Elements.RawText: data_rule_linear Constant rule

Elements.RawText: The parameters contain a `constant` number member. The value described by the constant rule is always equal to the

Lines.FullText: constant.

Elements.RawText: constant.
